titleStmt.title: Transcription and Translation of DCA DCD Misc. Ch. 626

respStmt.resp: Exported from Models of Authority data
respStmt.name: tei_exporter

availability.p: Status unknown. Exported for research and migration workflows.

revisionDesc.change: transcription moa=46 status=reviewed modified=2023-06-12 13:45:36.142887+00 | translation moa=45 status=reviewed modified=2023-06-12 13:45:36.160003+00

div.p:
Alexander . dei gratia rex Scottorum . omnibus probis hominibus tocius terre sue  salutem . Sciatis nos conces
sisse . deo et sancto Cuthberto de Coldingham’ . et monachis ibidem deo seruientibus . ut ipsi et eorum
seruientes per totam terram nostram liberi sint et quieti ab omni tolneyo de propriis catallis
et mercaturis eorum . Quare prohibemus . ne quis contra hanc libertatem eis infra terram nostram
iniuriam uel uiolentiam aliquam iniuste inferat super nostram forisfacturam . sicut carta domini regis .
Willelmi patris nostri testatur . Testibus . Willelmo de Boscho . cancellario . Willelmo Cumin . comite . de Buchan’ . iusticiario . scottorum . Alexandro . vicecomite . de Striuelin’ . Iohanne de Maccuswel’ . Apud Kinros’ . xxi . die Iulii . anno regni nostri octauo .

Alexander, by the grace of God king of Scots, to all worthy men of his whole land, greeting: Know that we have granted to God and to St Cuthbert at Coldingham and to the monks serving God there that they and their servants throughout our whole land be free and quit from all toll for their own chattels and merchandise. Wherefore we forbid that anyone unjustly bring any injury or violence on them contrary to this liberty within our land, upon our forfeiture, as the charter of the lord King William, our father, bears witness. As witnesses: William del Bois, chancellor; William Comyn, earl of Buchan, justiciar of the Scots; Alexander, sheriff of Stirling; John of Maxwell. At Kinross on the 21st day of July, in the eighth year of our reign.
